examples.title: Type Conversion Demonstration
examples.description: Program showing implicit and explicit type conversion in C++
examples.explanation: Demonstrates type conversion: implicit (automatic compiler conversion) and explicit (using cast operators). Shows how integer division differs from floating-point division.

examples.title: All Operators Demo
examples.description: Comprehensive program demonstrating different operator categories
examples.explanation: Demonstrates all major operator categories: arithmetic (+, -, *, /, %), relational (==, !=, >, <), logical (&&, ||, !), bitwise (&, |, ^, <<, >>), assignment (+=, -=, etc.), and ternary (? :)

examples.title: Increment/Decrement Operators
examples.description: Difference between pre and post increment/decrement
examples.explanation: Shows difference between pre-increment (++x) and post-increment (x++). Pre increments then returns new value; post returns current value then increments. The order of evaluation in expressions is important to understand.

examples.title: Storage Classes Demo
examples.description: Demonstrates different storage classes in C++
examples.explanation: Demonstrates storage classes: auto (automatic local), static (retains value), register (fast access), extern (external/global). Shows how static variables maintain state across function calls while auto variables are recreated each time.
